title: The Transformer Blueprint
concept: Transformers replaced LSTMs as the default backbone for NLP, paving the way for BERT and GPT. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Multi-Head Attention.'

exercises.prompt: Which architecture became the default blueprint for modern NLP models?
exercises.explain: Transformers replaced LSTMs as the standard architecture, leading to the creation of modern models like BERT and GPT.

cards.title: A New Era of Reading
cards.body: Before modern chatbots, computers read sentences like a slow reader: one word at a time, often forgetting the beginning of a long sentence. Then came the Transformer—a new blueprint that allowed AI to read entire pages at once!

cards.title: What is a Transformer?
cards.body: The Transformer is a neural network design that processes all words in a sentence simultaneously instead of one by one. It uses 'attention' to connect words, which lets it learn language much faster and handle massive amounts of text.

cards.title: The Desk Layout
cards.body: Imagine a translator reading a book. An LSTM is like a translator who reads word-by-word from left to right, slowly carrying notes to the next page. A Transformer is like a translator with a giant desk who lays out all the pages at once, looking at every word at the same time to understand the whole story instantly.

cards.title: Context in a Flash
cards.body: In the sentence 'The bank of the river was muddy,' a Transformer looks at 'river' and 'bank' at the same time. It immediately understands that 'bank' means the side of a river, not a place where money is kept, without needing to read the whole sentence step-by-step.

cards.title: Size Isn't Everything
cards.body: Many people think Transformers are better just because they are bigger. In reality, their secret power is their architecture: by looking at all words at once, they can be trained on huge datasets in parallel, something old models couldn't do.

cards.title: Step-by-Step vs. All-at-Once
cards.body: See how the processing style changed between the older LSTM models and the modern Transformer blueprint.

left.text: Read step-by-step. Slow, and easy to forget the beginning of long sentences.

right.text: Read all at once. Fast, and connects words across the entire text instantly.

exercises.prompt: What was a major limitation of LSTMs that led to the creation of the Transformer?

options.text: They were too fast for computers to run
They processed text step-by-step and struggled with long sentences
They only worked for math, not text
They were invented after GPT models

exercises.explain: LSTMs read text step-by-step, which makes them slow and prone to forgetting early words in long sentences. Transformers solved this.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Transformers process sentences one word at a time, from left to right.
exercises.explain: Transformers process all parts of the text at the same time, which is why they are so fast and powerful.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete how Transformers handle sentences.
exercises.sentence: Instead of reading word-by-word, Transformers process all words in [blank].
exercises.explain: Transformers process all words in parallel, which means they do it at the same time rather than waiting to read word-by-word.

exercises.prompt: How does a Transformer understand the meaning of a word in a sentence?

options.text: It translates one word and throws the rest of the text away.
It looks at all the words at the same time to find connections.
It reads the text backwards, starting from the last word.

exercises.explain: Transformers look at the whole sentence at once (in parallel) to understand the context of every word immediately.

exercises.prompt: Why did the Transformer architecture replace LSTMs as the default blueprint for language models?

options.text: They read text much slower than LSTMs
They process all words at the same time in parallel
They only work on tiny, single-word sentences
They forget the beginning of sentences entirely

exercises.explain: Transformers processed everything in parallel, bypassing the slow word-by-word path of LSTMs. This blueprint changed AI forever and paved the way for massive models like GPT! Next up: Multi-Head Attention.
